arts.ttl: Wednesday’s briefing: Newcastle take upper hand while West Ham line up Potter
arts.sum: The Magpies beat Arsenal 2-0 in their Carabao Cup semi-final first leg, with Tottenham taking on Liverpool on Wednesday evening.

arts.ttl: Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang names one player Arsenal need to win Premier League title
arts.sum: Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang's time at Arsenal ended acrimoniously after being punished by the club for multiple disciplinary breaches, with Mikel Arteta later stripping him of the captaincy

arts.ttl: Arsenal sent new transfer plea after painful Newcastle loss: 'Something needs to change'
arts.sum: Gunners’ need for attacking reinforcements made abundantly clear again in Carabao Cup semi-final first-leg defeat

arts.ttl: Isak and the ghost of Henry continue to haunt wasteful Arsenal
arts.sum: Alexander Isak stole all the headlines for Newcastle United, as Arsenal continue to deal with their wasted chances in front of goal.

arts.ttl: Premier League festive form table 2024: Newcastle shine, Man Utd & Chelsea awful
arts.sum: The Premier League period was crammed full of fixtures - some teams thrived while others struggled to keep up with the pace.

arts.ttl: Anthony Gordon rubs salt in Arsenal wounds as he recreates Thierry Henry's iconic corner flag celebration after scoring in Newcastle's 2-0 triumph at the Emirates
arts.sum: Anthony Gordon set tongues wagging during Newcastle's 2-0 victory over Arsenal on Tuesday night as he recreated Thierry Henry's iconic celebration.

arts.ttl: Arteta laments 'different' Cup ball in Arsenal loss
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has suggested Arsenal's failure to adapt to the Carabao Cup ball was a factor in Tuesday's 2-0 semifinal, first leg defeat to Newcastle United.

arts.ttl: Theo Walcott reveals what worries him the most about Arsenal as his old club lose 2-0 in semi-final first leg against Newcastle... while Jamie Redknapp labels the Gunners 'toothless' without injured Bukayo Saka
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta's side have been left with a mammoth task if they want to reach the final of the competition after losing 2-0 at the Emirates in their semi-final first leg tie on Tuesday night.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta bizarrely blames the Carabao Cup BALL for Arsenal's missed chances against Newcastle... as he bemoans his side 'kicking a lot over the bar' during their 2-0 defeat in the first leg
arts.sum: ISAAN KHAN AT THE EMIRATES: Mike Arteta bizarrely brought up the Carabao Cup's Puma match ball when breaking down Arsenal's 2-0 defeat by Newcastle.

arts.ttl: Isak’s razor-sharp edge provides painful point of contrast to Arsenal’s meanderings | Barney Ronay
arts.athr: Barney Ronay at the Emirates Stadium
arts.sum: Newcastle striker isn’t just a player Arsenal have wanted, but a type they have missed – there isn’t a better centre-forward in England right now And here’s what you could have had. No, maybe don’t look too closely. It is no secret Arsenal made attempts to sign Alexander Isak from Real Sociedad a few years back. There was some vague, whisper-on-the-wind talk of a bid again this summer. Good luck with that one now. How much would it take these days? A hundred and fifty million? A six pack of gold Fabergé eggs encased in Parmesan cheese? Ten thousand head of cattle and half of Hertfordshire? Isak isn’t just a player Arsenal have wanted, but a type they have missed, a razor edge in a team that still basically wants to tickle you to death. No doubt after this 2-0 semi-final first leg victory for an excellent Newcastle team, driven by a wonderful performance from Isak, there will be some painful focus on the points of contrast with Arsenal’s own meanderings in attack. Continue reading...
